title: West Elm Knock Off Media Cabinet
summary: Make a media cabinet to fit perfectly underneath your family room windows.
description: Make a media cabinet to fit perfectly underneath your family room windows. It is a really easy build and the two tone look gives it the high end look.

steps.title: Pocketholes
steps.body: 1. Drill pocketholes into the sides on the 16” side both at the top and bottom. Repeat for the other side.

steps.title: Pocketholes in the Shelf
steps.body: 2. Drill pocketholes into the shelf on the 18” side on both the left and right side of the piece of wood.

steps.title: Legs
steps.body: 3. To cut the legs take a 2 x 4 scrap and cut a 10 degree angle. Then cut a 4” leg. Repeat that three more times.

steps.title: Leg Pocketholes
steps.body: 4. Drill 2 pocketholes into the legs going from the shorter edge into the longer edge.

steps.title: Veneer Edging
steps.body: 5. Add veneer edging to all of the plywood edges that will be shown using an iron. Use a veneer trimmer to clean up the edges.

steps.title: Shelf Placement
steps.body: 6. Drill your shelf into the height that you want it.

steps.title: Euro Hinges
steps.body: 7. Drill your holes for your euro hinges using a euro hinge jig.

steps.title: Assembling Cabinet
steps.body: 8. To assemble the cabinet drill the sides into the top and bottom insetting them in 1/4” from the back and 3/4” from the front.

steps.title: Adding Feet
steps.body: 9. Flip your cabinet over and add your feet to the bottom making sure the pocketholes are facing the back.

steps.title: Doors
steps.body: 10. Add your doors. Drill your holes for your knobs.
